title: The post-May '68 Christian philosophy now being embraced by the American far right
subhead: Trump supporters often blame the cultural upheavals triggered by the May 1968 protests in France for sowing the seeds of 'wokeness.' Yet some of them are now claiming a different French school of thought, which took shape at Stanford University under the influence of René Girard.

Roland-Garros 2026: New crop of French tennis talents emerges
subhead: Moïse Kouamé and Ksenia Efremova are two of France's most exciting emerging tennis talents. But at just 17 years old, they will need to learn to balance promising skills and intense scrutiny.

title: Cannes Film Festival: Cristian Mungiu wins Palme d'Or for 'Fjord' at closing ceremony overshadowed by the state of the world
subhead: On May 23, the jury, chaired by South Korean filmmaker Park Chan-wook, awarded the Romanian director with the Palme d'Or for the second time, and presented its Grand Prix to 'Minotaur' by the Russian director Andrey Zvyagintsev.

title: Russian bombardment of Kyiv kills four
subhead: The attacks came as retaliation after Ukrainian strikes in Russian-occupied eastern Ukraine killed 21 students.

title: Economist Guillaume Vallet: 'The Enhanced Games glorifies a certain type of man, dominant and celebrated by MAGA ideology'
subhead: In an interview, the economics professor analyzes this competition between doped athletes, scheduled for May 24 in Las Vegas. Advocating for an unregulated market logic, these unprecedented Games in the history of sport introduce a new paradigm of confrontation, outside the democratic framework.

Tamil cinema superstar Vijay makes spectacular political debut in India
subhead: Actor Joseph Vijay Chandrasekhar has stunned the country by winning regional elections in Tamil Nadu. The 51-year-old relied on his millions of fans and campaigned on a platform of change.

title: What we know about the Paris brawl involving Nice supporters ahead of French Cup final
subhead: A major brawl broke out in Paris's 10th arrondissement, where around 100 supporters of OGC Nice, who are set to face RC Lens in the French Cup final, were gathered on Thursday evening. Sixty-five people were arrested.
